page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores utilizing the 64-bit ARM64 architecture, specifically the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top tier. Large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is strong at 86.14 GB/sec, while read bandwidth reaches 153.96 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance. However, the ARMv9 architecture shows performance tradeoffs in specific workloads. The Passmark CPU encryption test is weak, and large-model LLM inference is unsupported due to memory constraints. Furthermore, LLM inference prompt processing for medium models is poor at 15.62 tokens/sec, and text generation for small models is weak at 48.61 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gn.2xlarge delivers high memory density per vCPU without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware, making it a resource-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require cryptographic acceleration or heavy AI inference. The instance is suited for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, text processing, and memory-intensive web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz and 64.0 GB of system memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this server provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 86.14 GB/sec. However, tradeoffs exist, including weak performance in CPU encryption and small-model LLM text generation, as well as poor prompt processing speeds for medium LLM models. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, Redis caching, and text processing workloads that benefit from high memory density and dedicated ARM cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.2xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server with 8 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized ARM64 virtual server featuring dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
